info.name: OneTwenty — Python Backend API
info.description:
All HTTP API endpoints for the OneTwenty multi-tenant OneTwenty backend.

Base URL: https://{{slug}}.onetwenty.dev/api/v1

Variables:
  base_url   = https://onetwenty.dev
  slug       = ayush  (your tenant slug)
  access_token  = <JWT access token from /auth/login>
  api_secret_hash = <SHA-1 hash of your API secret>

request.description: Register a new user account. Returns user_id on success.

Authenticate and receive JWT access + refresh tokens.

The test script auto-saves access_token and refresh_token to collection variables.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access token.
Get the existing API secret for the user's tenant, or create one if none exists. Requires JWT.
Revoke the current API secret and generate a new one. ⚠️ All uploaders using the old secret will break.
Get current user's profile including tenant info and subdomain URL.
Update user profile details (onboarding data).
Fetch server status and tenant configuration. No auth needed — tenant resolved from subdomain.
Fetch status authenticated via API secret header.
Fetch status authenticated via JWT Bearer token.
Update tenant settings. Accepts partial updates — only provided fields are merged.
Fetch the latest 10 entries. Tenant resolved from subdomain — no auth header needed.
OneTwenty find[] query syntax. Filters entries where sgv ≥ 120.
Filter entries by type (mbg = manual blood glucose).
Latest SGV entry. Returns JSON array by default.
Latest SGV entry in TSV format: dateString\tdate\tsgv\tdirection\tdevice
Fetch a single entry by its MongoDB ObjectId. Replace the ID in the URL.
Filter entries by type. Try: sgv, mbg, cal.
Upload a single CGM entry. Upserts by (sysTime, type) — safe to retry.
Upload multiple CGM entries in one request.
Delete a single entry by its ObjectId.
Delete all entries of a specific type (sgv, mbg, cal, etc.).
Delete all entries matching a find[] filter. Example: delete entries older than a timestamp.

item.name: GET Entries with Events — Time Range

request.description: Fetch both CGM entries and events for the specified time range.
Doctor assigns a patient by their email. Requires doctor role.
Doctor lists all their assigned patients.
Patient lists all doctors who have access to their data.
Patient revokes a doctor's access. Replace 7 with the doctor's user ID.
Fetch all events (treatments) for the tenant.
Create a new event/treatment.
Delete a treatment by its ObjectId.

Send natural language text to the OneTwenty AI Assistant. Upgraded to Claude 3 Haiku for improved accuracy. Uses a compact context format (TOON-like) to reduce token usage by ~80% while seeing more history. Extracts events like carbs and insulin automatically.

query.description: User local offset in minutes (e.g., -330 for IST)

request.description: Upload an audio file to be transcribed by AWS Transcribe and processed by Claude 3 Haiku. Includes optimized historical context for accurate insights.
Retrieve previous conversations with the AI assistant.

Generate a professional PDF report. This automatically triggers the AI Clinical Summary (Bedrock) to provide personalized insights (Wins & Focus Areas) based on your glucose data. Returns a pre-signed S3 URL.
List all previously generated reports for the tenant.
Get dashboard metrics and AGP chart data.
